name: sqlitebrowser
title: sqlitebrowser
description:
GUI editor for SQLite databases
SQLite Database Browser is a visual tool used to create, design and edit
database files compatible with SQLite. Its interface is based on QT,
and is meant to be used for users and developers that want to create
databases, edit and search data using a familiar spreadsheet-like
interface, without the need to learn complicated SQL commands.
Controls and wizards are available for users to:
- Create and compact database files
- Create, define, modify and delete tables
- Create, define and delete indexes
- Browse, edit, add and delete records
- Search records
- Import and export records as text
- Import and export tables from/to CSV files
- Import and export databases from/to SQL dump files
- Issue SQL queries and inspect the results
- Examine a log of all SQL commands issued by the application
.
SQLite Database Browser is not a visual shell for the sqlite command line
tool. It does not require familiarity with SQL commands.

commands.name: sqlitebrowser
commands.description: light GUI editor for SQLite databases

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sqlitebrowser because GUI editor for SQLite databases.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sqlitebrowser as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqlitebrowser [file]` as documented upstream for sqlitebrowser.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sqlitebrowser with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sqlitebrowser --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: sqlitebrowser - light GUI editor for SQLite databases
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: GUI editor for SQLite databases.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: sqlitebrowser - light GUI editor for SQLite databases.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install sqlitebrowser
